response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack and distributed systems engineer with a solid track record across product and infrastructure work, but he is not a match for this role. The most disqualifying gap is C++: the role requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet Bogdan's resume shows only a brief ~6-month C++ engagement at National Instruments and a thesis project — C++ does not even appear in his skills list. Beyond the language requirement, his background is predominantly web, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang, with no substantive cloud compute platform or infrastructure leadership experience. His distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic is relevant but is roughly 2 years old and was as an individual contributor, not a technical lead.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic) shows genuine infrastructure instinct
Consistent application of engineering best practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — across multiple senior and staff-level roles
Broad technical breadth (embedded, mobile, web, DevOps tooling) and a current Staff Engineer role at Stripe suggest strong adaptability and ceiling
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments) against a hard requirement of 8 years — this alone disqualifies the candidate
No evidence of technical leadership over cloud or infrastructure teams; leadership experience is primarily mentorship and craftsmanship advocacy, not platform ownership
Cloud architecture depth is thin: AWS CDK usage at Shopify and Kubernetes mention for a personal project do not constitute production cloud compute platform experience
